zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Hay於大約1476年出生在英國時，她的父親William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll32歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Leslie Countess of Erroll32歲。她於1499年與David Lindsay 8th Earl of Crawford在蘇格蘭藍納可爾克勞福德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1511年1月24日享年36歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Hay于大约1476年出生在英国时，她的父亲William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll32岁，母亲Lady Elizabeth Leslie Countess of Erroll32岁。她于1499年与David Lindsay 8th Earl of Crawford在苏格兰藍納可爾克勞福德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1511年1月24日享年36岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: High
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Abdul, Syed, Arifa, Atta, Faiz, Hamid, Iftekhar, Iskandar, Mohamed, Mohammad, Mohannad.  Vietnamese Mui, Pham, Tran, Vo, Bui, Cao, Chau, Dinh, Duong, Nguyen, Sau, Vo Van.  Jewish Ilan, Ronen, Shlomo.  Chinese Chan, Chiming, Lung, Wai Fun, Yin.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): from a personal name based on Arabic ḥayy ‘alive’. Al-Ḥayy ‘the Living’ is an attribute of Allah.
Jewish: variant of   Hay  .

variants.name: Dehay
meanings: French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the masculine form of Old French haye ‘hedge’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word (see Hay 9).
Altered form (also De Hay) of Dutch De Haij or De Haaij, which is probably of French origin (see above).

variants.name: Haig
meanings: Scottish and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named with Old Norse hagi ‘enclosure’, a word with cognates in most ancient Germanic languages. Compare Hay . The Haigs of Bemersyde in Berwickshire derive their name from the district of La Hague in Manche.
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thaoig and Mac Thaidhg (see McCaig ).

variants.name: Hayman
meanings: English: topographic name probably from Middle English hai(e), hei(e) ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg), denoting a dweller by an enclosure (see Hay 2 ), or else a nickname for a ‘keeper of the enclosure’ (compare Hayward ).
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, high(e) (Old English hēah); see also Hay 3.
English: occupational name for a seller of hay, from Middle English hai, hei (Old English hēg, hīg).

variants.name: Hayes
meanings: Irish (Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAodha ‘descendant of Aodh’, a personal name meaning ‘fire’. Compare McCoy . In some cases especially in County Wexford, the surname is of English origin (see below), having been taken to Ireland by the Normans.
English: variant of Hay , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: topographic name from the plural form of Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (see Hay ), sometimes used as a collective noun for a farm, especially in Devon, where it is a frequent minor placename. Compare Hain .

variants.name: Haycock
meanings: English: from a pet form of the Middle English personal name Hai + coc; see Hay 4 and compare Cocke .

variants.name: Haylett
meanings: English (Norfolk): from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Hai (see Hay 4), from Hai + -el + -ot.

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Hay was born about 1476, in England, her father, William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll, was 32 and her mother, Lady Elizabeth Leslie Countess of Erroll, was 32. She married David Lindsay 8th Earl of Crawford in 1499, in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 24 January 1511, at the age of 36.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Hay est née environ 1476, à Angleterre, son père, William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Lady Elizabeth Leslie Countess of Erroll, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé David Lindsay 8th Earl of Crawford en 1499, à Crawford, Lanarkshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 24 janvier 1511, à l’âge de 36 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Hay um 1476 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll 32 und ihre Mutter Lady Elizabeth Leslie Countess of Erroll 32. Sie hat 1499 in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland David Lindsay 8th Earl of Crawford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 24. Januar 1511 im Alter von 36 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Hay è nata il circa 1476 nel England, sua padre, William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Lady Elizabeth Leslie Countess of Erroll, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato David Lindsay 8th Earl of Crawford nel 1499, nel Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 24 gennaio 1511, all'età di 36 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hayが約1476年にイギリスで生まれた時，父William Hay 3rd Earl of Errollは32歳，母Lady Elizabeth Leslie Countess of Errollは32歳であった。1499年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアクラウフォードでDavid Lindsay 8th Earl of Crawfordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1511年1月24日に36歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hay(이)가 대략 1476년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Leslie Countess of Erroll(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1499년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 크롤퍼드에서 David Lindsay 8th Earl of Crawford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1511년 1월 24일 36세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Hay nasceu aproximadamente 1476, em Inglaterra, seu pai, William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Lady Elizabeth Leslie Countess of Erroll, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com David Lindsay 8th Earl of Crawford em 1499, em Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 24 de janeiro de 1511, com 36 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Hay родилась около 1476 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Leslie Countess of Erroll, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени David Lindsay 8th Earl of Crawford в 1499 г. в местности кравфорд, ланаркшир, Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 24 января 1511 г. в возрасте 36 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Hay nació aproximadamente 1476, en Inglaterra, su papá, William Hay 3rd Earl of Erroll, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Lady Elizabeth Leslie Countess of Erroll, tenía 32 años. Se casó con David Lindsay 8th Earl of Crawford en 1499, en Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 24 de enero de 1511, a la edad de 36 años.
